name: KGB Museum
description: "There's nothing there," and other lies the KGB told us.

name: Ichthus Art Gallery
description: A secluded art gallery hidden within the cellars beneath Tallinn’s medieval monastery.

name: Rummu Underwater Prison
description: The ruins of an Estonian prison are drowning in the quarry lake where the convicts were once forced to work.

name: Linnahall
description: An empty relic of the Moscow Olympics crumbles away in Estonia's capital.

name: Kaali Meteorite Crater Field
description: Mysterious craters may have been home to an ancient cult.

name: Seaplane Harbour
description: Disused seaplane hangar gets a second life as the jaw-dropping home of Estonia's museum of maritime history.

name: Telliskivi Creative City
description: Estonia's largest creative city is a vibrant bohemian hub located within a former industrial complex.

name: Tuhala Witch's Well
description: This disappearing geyser has been attributed to witchcraft, but really it's all about rainfall.

name: Tallinn Kiek in de Kök
description: This medieval Estonian tower provides access to a series of tunnels that have been home to soldiers, punks, and fine art.

name: Pähni Megaphones
description: A trio of giant wooden megaphones turn up nature's volume in this Estonian forest.

name: Raeapteek
description: The oldest continuously operating pharmacy in Europe.

name: St Nicholas' Church Danse Macabre
description: Only a fragment of this 15th-century Dance of Death survives today.

name: Soviet Statue Graveyard
description: Relics from an era long gone, sit idle outside this Estonian museum.

name: Haunted Guest House
description: Spend your honeymoon where the Devil had his wedding.

name: Glehn Park
description: A forest park outside Tallinn full of architectural and artistic curiosities.

name: Ungru Manor
description: The gutted remains of this Estonian mansion are a monument to a tragic love.

name: Piusa Sand Caves
description: Man-made mineral caves house massive bat colony.

name: St Catherine's Passage
description: A medieval setting reimagined into a unique art alley.

name: Museum of Soviet Vehicles
description: Rusted cars, trucks, and buses form a fascinating collection of Soviet-era machinery.

name: Maarjamäe Memorial Complex
description: This striking memorial is one of Estonia’s most controversial Soviet leftovers.

name: Hara Submarine Base
description: A decommissioned submarine base makes a great space for graffiti art.

name: Sillamäe
description: This Soviet-era "closed town" was a top secret uranium producer often left off of maps.

name: Banned Books Museum
description: This free museum is building a collection of books that are banned, censored or burnt around the world.

name: Kingdom of Torgu
description: A clerical error gave rise to this imaginary micronation in the southwestern corner of Estonia.

name: Epitaphs of The Cathedral of Saint Mary
description: The oldest church in the Estonian capital is home to over 100 fantastical cemetery coats-of-arms.

name: KuKu Club
description: An underground nightclub helped spark a "Singing Revolution."

name: Kiipsaare Lighthouse
description: Once standing on solid ground, this slender beacon is a victim of the very shoreline it was charged with announcing.

name: Brown Bear Watching Hide
description: Two well-hidden huts let animal lovers spend the night among the bears, wolves, and other predators of the forest.

name: Hill of Crosses (Estonia)
description: This small Estonian hill is covered in hand-made twig crosses that are said to bring good luck.

name: Fortress Patarei
description: A former imperial sea-fortress and Soviet prison.

name: Pärnu Seawall
description: Legend says lovers who hold hands while venturing along this stone jetty and kiss at the end will remain together forever.

name: Old Thomas
description: The Guardian of Tallinn.

name: Museum of KGB Cells
description: The secret cells in the basement of the KGB's Estonian "Grey House" have been preserved as a grim Cold War museum.

name: Hellemann Tower and City Walls
description: Walk along the medieval fortifications that have been protecting Tallinn since the 14th century.

name: Military Structures of Hiiumaa
description: Wars fought and wars won are remembered by these abandoned bunker ruins.

name: Kadriorg Palace
description: A spectacular Petrine Baroque summer palace commissioned for Catherine I by Peter the Great.

name: Tallinn Teletorn (TV Tower)
description: This dizzying tower was built to better broadcast the 1980 Moscow Olympics.

name: Tallinn Museum of Photography
description: This former medieval prison now unlocks Estonia’s rich photographic history.

name: 'Cromatico'
description: This set of concrete chambers resonates according to different notes of octaves.

name: Ämari Pilots’ Cemetery
description: This soviet pilot's graveyard is filled with headstones made of airplane fins.

name: Noora
description: The labyrinthine home of the National Archives of Estonia is an experimental blend of architecture, art, and history.

name: Kohtla Mining Park
description: This old Estonian mine has been kept like a sprawling time capsule of the local shale industry.

name: Turisalu Missile Hangar
description: Abandoned Soviet base once stocked with short and medium range missiles pointed at western Europe.

name: Saatse Boot
description: This peculiar border irregularity is the only way to legally enter Russia without a visa.

name: Jägala Waterfall
description: The widest natural waterfall in Estonia is a sight to see in all seasons.

name: Haapsalu Airfield
description: This abandoned fighter jet launch center is now a haunting street racing course.

name: Madise's Lost Cemetery
description: Tombstones buried in overgrown grass mark the deceased occupants of this abandoned graveyard.

name: The Chopin Bench
description: Take a seat and hear a song by the composer Frédéric Chopin.

name: Saint Olaf's Church
description: During the Soviet era, the spire of this longstanding church was used as a radio transmission tower.

name: The Bronze Soldier of Tallinn
description: This stoic bronze soldier stands as a symbol of Russian sacrifice or Soviet oppression, or maybe both.

name: Valaste Waterfall
description: This stunning limestone waterfall is the highest in Estonia.

name: Tallinn Black Angus Sculpture
description: A unique sculpture calls the outside of this steakhouse home.

name: Vormsi Cemetery
description: Hundreds of unusual wheel crosses poke above the earth in this small Estonian cemetery.

name: Väike-Õismäe District
description: A series of concentric circles represented a bold new experiment in socialist urban planning, and it notoriously difficult to get around.

name: Estonian Open Air Museum
description: A massive tribute to Estonian history and culture.

name: Valga/Valka Border
description: For the last century, this international border within a small town has appeared and disappeared various times.

name: Kihnu Island
description: This little gem in the Gulf of Riga has a very rich heritage and distinct culture.

name: Okta Centrum AS
description: This abandoned office building is a lasting example of Soviet-era architecture.

name: 'Lucky Chimney Sweeper'
description: Rubbing the buttons along this statue's coat is considered good luck.

name: Kolkja Kala-ja Sibularestora (Kolkja Old Believers Restaurant)
description: This unique eatery offers a taste of Estonia's "Onion Route."

name: The Tree Crosses of Rosma Forest
description: Hundreds of small crosses have been carved into the bark of the trees outside this small Estonian village.

name: Supilinn - The Soup Town
description: Historic slum of Tartu.

name: Peko
description: A wooden statue of an Estonian pagan god and King of the Setos stands atop a sacred hill.

name: Samovar House
description: Learn to drink tea like an Estonian at this museum.

name: Old Observatory in Tartu
description: First point in the Struve Geodetic Arc, a 19th century undertaking to measure the Earth's size and shape.

name: Kõpu Lighthouse
description: One of the oldest lighthouses in the world has been in continuous use since it was first built in 1531.

name: Hiiumaa Eiffel Tower
description: A wooden replica of France's famous tower.

name: Seegi Maja Restaurant
description: This atmospheric spot in Pärnu's historic old town is located in a 17th-century almshouse.

name: The Struve Geodetic Arc
description: A historic scientific breakthrough that crosses nine borders.

name: Karja Church
description: Inside this medieval church resides a unique set of murals.

name: Old Believers Museum
description: Its collection offers a glimpse into the Russian Old Believers' centuries-old way of life.

name: Chicory Museum
description: See tools once used for processing this coffee-substitute plant before sampling its flavor firsthand.

name: Kissing Hill
description: This park built on a former fortress has a reputation for romance.

name: Ruhnu Lighthouse
description: It's rumored this unique Baltic beacon was designed by Gustave Eiffel himself.

name: Kalamaja Cemetery  Park
description: This 16th-century cemetery became a recreational park during the Soviet occupation—today it's a little bit of both.

name: Tartu Student Lock-Up
description: Troublesome students were detained in this tiny graffiti-covered university jail.

name: The "Russalka" Memorial
description: The first monument in Estonia that was made by an Estonian sculptor.

name: World’s Largest Hoberman Sphere
description: The enormous isokinetic structure hangs within a science center in Estonia.

name: Alatskivi Castle
description: A wealthy aristocratic family built this Disney-worthy, fairy-tale castle.

name: Kreenholm Manufacturing Company
description: An abandoned textile factory on the "island of crows" was home to Estonia's first workers' strike.

name: Pesapuu Watchtower
description: This Estonian watchtower looks like a big tree with two giant bird nests on it, complete with a "golden egg."

name: Ninase Windmills
description: Two old windmills got a new life as oversized sculptures of a man and a woman.

name: Lasva Water Tower
description: This old and disused water tower has received a new life as an art gallery with a piano staircase.

name: Rakvere Aurochs Statue
description: This behemoth bovine guards an Estonian town and is a memorial to the lost species.

name: Charles Leroux Memorial
description: Dedicated to the untimely death of American balloonist and parachutist.

name: The Famous Rock of Tondi
description: A large boulder was declared a local landmark by a popular vlogger.

name: Orissaare Oak
description: Legend has it that not even Stalin could pull down the 2015 European Tree of the Year.

name: 'Soorebane' ('Bog Fox')
description: The first designer power line in Estonia.

name: Lehmja Oak Forest
description: An ancient oak forest where, legend has it, a wedding party was magically transformed into trees.

name: Telise Marker Towers
description: These two strange cones are actually a system of old nautical navigation beacons.

name: Karl Ernst von Baer Statue
description: Once a year, local students "clean" this statue with a champagne shower.

name: Monument to Paul Keres
description: Visitors can sit and study the board with this Estonian chess legend and International Grandmaster.

name: Communication and Laptop Museum
description: A small museum showcasing various communication equipment and laptops in the middle of the southeast Estonia countryside.

name: Railway and Communications Museum
description: Explore 150 years of Estonian rail history in a terminal once built for Tsar Nicholas II.

name: Narva Lenin
description: A statue of the Soviet leader still stands in a quiet corner of Estonia’s most Russian city.
